docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All requests require an API key in the header:

Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY

docs.title: GET /status
docs.text:
Returns the current status of the coffee machine.

Request

GET /status
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY

Response

{
  "status": "idle",
  "water_level": "full",
  "bean_level": "medium",
  "last_brew": "2026-06-05T08:30:00Z"
}
Field Type Description status string Machine state: idle, brewing, error water_level string Water level: full, medium, low bean_level string Bean level: full, medium, low last_brew string ISO 8601 timestamp of last brew

docs.title: POST /brew
docs.text:
Starts a brew cycle.

Request

POST /brew
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "strength": "strong",
  "cups": 2
}

Response

{
  "brew_id": "abc123",
  "estimated_time": 180,
  "status": "brewing"
}

Note

Maximum 4 cups per brew cycle.

docs.title: API Key
docs.text: To authenticate API requests, you need an API key.

docs.title: Generate an API key
docs.text:
1. Open the Smart Coffee Machine mobile app.
  2. Go to Settings → Developer Options.
  3. Tap Generate API Key.
  4. Copy the key and store it securely.

Warning

Your API key is shown only once. If you lose it, you must generate a new one.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Store your API key as an environment variable instead of hardcoding it:

export COFFEE_API_KEY="your_api_key_here"

docs.title: Configuration File
docs.text:
You can also use a config.yaml file:

api:
  key: your_api_key_here
  base_url: https://api.smartcoffee.io/v1
  timeout: 30

device:
  name: Kitchen Coffee Machine
  model: X200

Note

The configuration file must be in the same directory as your application.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Smart Coffee Machine API lets you control your coffee machine remotely, schedule brews, and monitor status from any device.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before you begin, make sure you have:

  • A Smart Coffee Machine (Model X200 or later)
  • A valid API key (see Configuration)
  • Python 3.8+ or any REST client (curl, Postman)

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
1. Connect your coffee machine to Wi-Fi using the mobile app.
  2. Generate your API key in the device settings.
  3. Make your first API call:
curl -X GET https://api.smartcoffee.io/v1/status \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY"

docs.title: Machine not responding
docs.text:
Symptom: API returns connection timeout or 503 Service Unavailable.

Solutions:

  1. Check that the coffee machine is powered on.
  2. Verify the machine is connected to Wi-Fi.
  3. Restart the machine and wait 30 seconds before retrying.

Tip

You can check connectivity status in the mobile app under Settings → Network.

docs.title: Invalid API key error
docs.text:
Symptom: API returns 401 Unauthorized.

Solutions:

  • Confirm you're using the correct API key.
  • Check that the key hasn't expired.
  • Generate a new key in Settings → Developer Options.

docs.title: Brew cycle not starting
docs.text:
Symptom: POST /brew returns 409 Conflict.

Possible causes:

Error code Meaning Action MACHINE_BUSY Already brewing Wait for current cycle to finish LOW_WATER Water tank empty Refill water tank LOW_BEANS Bean hopper empty Refill bean hopper DOOR_OPEN Lid not closed Close the machine lid

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
If the issue persists, contact support:

  • Email: support@smartcoffee.io
  • Community forum: community.smartcoffee.io
